tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I've been thinking a lot lately about my creative direction with ceramics. I find myself really drawn to the way Syd Carpenter explores themes of nature and identity in her work—there's such a quiet strength to it. I'd love to capture this somewhere so I can keep my artistic preferences and inspiration sources organized. Could you help me store this as a reference for my creative process?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Creative inspiration reference: Drawn to Syd Carpenter's ceramics work - appreciates the way she explores themes of nature and identity. Finds there to be a \"quiet strength\" in Carpenter's work. Wants to keep this artistic preference organized as a reference for their own creative process with ceramics."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Creative inspiration reference: Drawn to Syd Carpenter's ceramics work - appreciates the way she explores themes of nature and identity. Finds there to be a \"quiet strength\" in Carpenter's work. Wants to keep this artistic preference organized as a reference for their own creative process with ceramics."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Done! I've stored your inspiration from Syd Carpenter—her exploration of nature and identity, and that "quiet strength" you connect with—as a reference for your ceramics practice. It'll be there when you want to revisit it as you develop your creative direction.
That's great, thank you! Now I'm curious—could you show me what you've stored so far? I'd like to see my artistic preferences and inspiration sources that you've captured.

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Creative inspiration reference: Drawn to Syd Carpenter's ceramics work - appreciates the way she explores themes of nature and identity. Finds there to be a \"quiet strength\" in Carpenter's work. Wants to keep this artistic preference organized as a reference for their own creative process with ceramics."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what you've stored so far:

**Your artistic inspiration reference:**
- **Syd Carpenter** – You're drawn to her ceramics work and the way she explores themes of nature and identity. You find "quiet strength" in her work and want to keep this as a reference for your own creative direction with ceramics.

That's the only memory in storage right now. If you'd like to add more inspiration sources or artistic preferences as you continue reflecting on your creative direction, just let me know!

That's exactly right—thank you for showing me. The capture looks perfect: my appreciation for Syd Carpenter's exploration of nature and identity in her ceramics, and that sense of quiet strength I find so inspiring. It's all there just as I wanted it. I think I'm all set for now.

Wonderful! I'm glad it captured exactly what you wanted. Your creative inspiration is now safely stored and ready to reference whenever you need it. Feel free to reach out anytime you want to add more to your artistic collection or explore your creative direction further. Best of luck with your ceramics work!

I find myself really drawn to the way Syd Carpenter explores themes of nature and identity in her work—there's such a quiet strength to it. I'd love to capture this somewhere so I can keep my artistic preferences and inspiration sources organized. Could you help me store this as a reference for my creative process?
